key_metrics: $137.5 billion core net new client assets
qoq_changes: Daily average trades (DATs) declined slightly from the second quarter of 2025
new_risks: Lower yields on interest-earning assets due to a 25 basis point Federal Reserve rate cut in September
Net outflows of $3.1 billion from off-platform brokered certificates of deposit (CDs)
management_tone: Bullish, as management highlighted strong client asset gathering, sustained client engagement, and record equity market levels driving significant revenue and earnings growth.
analyst_note: Schwab delivered a highly profitable Q3 2025 with net income surging 67% YoY, fueled by $137.5 billion in core net new assets, reduced reliance on costly supplemental funding, and strong equity markets, positioning the firm well despite a shifting interest rate environment.
